user_query: Update fathers maple kissed taco option to Birria tacos

updates.tree_path: Root > MealPlan_Version 5 > Father > Day 7 > Dinner > DIY Taco Night — Maple-Kissed Skillet

meal_suggestion.from: Assemble made‑to‑order tacos: warm corn tortillas, top with lean beef, shredded lettuce, diced tomato, cheddar, and salsa. Optional lime, avocado, or light sour cream. Pair with a simple side salad or beans for extra fiber.
meal_suggestion.to: Dip corn tortillas into the top layer of the birria broth (consomé) and sear on a hot griddle. Fill with tender, shredded slow-cooked beef and melted Oaxaca cheese. Fold and cook until crispy. Serve with a small bowl of warm consomé for dipping, topped with fresh cilantro and diced white onions.

short_description.from: A quick, customizable taco night featuring lean beef, corn tortillas, fresh veggies, cheddar, and salsa.

nutrition_note.from: Lean beef provides high‑quality protein; corn tortillas supply carbohydrates; lettuce and tomato add volume and micronutrients; cheddar contributes calcium and fat; salsa offers flavor with relatively low calories. To boost fiber, use whole‑grain tortillas or add beans; to reduce sodium, opt for low‑sodium salsa and lighter cheese.
nutrition_note.to: Birria is rich in protein and iron from the slow-cooked beef. Using corn tortillas keeps the meal gluten-free and provides complex carbohydrates. While higher in sodium due to the broth, it can be balanced by using low-sodium stock and plenty of fresh cilantro and onion for micronutrients.

updates.reasoning: Transformed the 'Maple-Kissed' taco option into Birria tacos as requested. Updated the name, description, ingredients, and meal suggestion to reflect the specific preparation style of Birria.
